column2: Increase peak learning rate with extended warmup
Push peak learning rates to 2x baseline with 10% warmup
Try 1.75x peak learning rates (midpoint between 1.5x keep and 2x discard)
Reduce warmup ratio from 10% to 5% (more time at peak LR)
column4: positive — val_bpb improved from 0.995725 to 0.995672 (δ = -0.000053). reducing warmup_ratio from 0.10 to 0.05 gives more training steps at peak lr within the 300s budget. small but real improvement from a minimal one-line change. committed and pushed to autoresearch/run1.

column2: Reduce cooldown fraction to spend more steps near peak LR
Increase weight decay to complement the 1.5x higher learning rates
Reduce weight decay from 0.2 to 0.1 (less regularization with 1.5x LR)
column4: positive — val_bpb improved from 0.995672 to 0.995575 (−0.000097). reducing weight decay from 0.2 to 0.1 helped the model fit better with the 1.5x higher lr providing sufficient implicit regularization. zero complexity change, kept and pushed.

column2: Tune Muon optimizer momentum beta (try reducing from default toward 0.85-0.90)
Increase model width (hidden dimension) by ~20% to use available VRAM headroom
Further reduce weight decay from 0.1 to 0.05 (continue less-regularization trend)
Increase model depth by 1 layer (test depth scaling vs width scaling)
column4: positive — val_bpb improved from 0.995575 → 0.994722 (delta: -0.000853). depth scaling (8→9 layers) is more compute-efficient than width scaling: only +20% step time vs +40%+ for width. kept and pushed to autoresearch/run1.

column2: Add another layer (9→10) to continue depth scaling
column4: negative — increasing depth from 9 to 10 worsened val_bpb from 0.994722 to 1.001365 (δ = +0.006643). the build_model_config function scales n_embd = ceil(depth * aspect_ratio / head_dim) * head_dim, so depth 9→10 also triggered a width jump from 512 to 640 (and n_head 4→5). this combined depth+width increase made the model too large (85.9m params, 68gb vram, 536ms/step, only 571 steps in 5 min). the reduced step count hurt more than the added capacity helped. reverted train.py, no push.

column2: Increase depth to 10 while reducing ASPECT_RATIO to prevent width jump (keep n_embd=512)
Increase Muon final momentum target from 0.95 to 0.975 (push high-momentum trend)
column4: negative — increasing muon final momentum from 0.95 to 0.975 degraded val_bpb significantly: 0.997449 vs baseline 0.994722 (+0.002727). the current 0.95 is better than both higher (0.975) and lower (0.90) values, confirming it is near-optimal. reverted train.py, no code pushed.

column2: Tune sliding window attention size (explore SSSL/window parameters)
column4: positive — halving sliding window size from seq_len//2 (1024) to seq_len//4 (512) improved val_bpb from 0.994722 to 0.993732. simple 1-line change, no vram or complexity increase.

column2: Further reduce sliding window size from seq_len//4 (512) to seq_len//8 (256)
column4: positive — reducing sliding window from seq_len//4 (512) to seq_len//8 (256) improved val_bpb from 0.993732 to 0.993565 (δ=-0.000167). simple 1-line change, no complexity added. vram slightly decreased. committed and pushed to autoresearch/run1.

column2: Further reduce sliding window size from seq_len//8 (256) to seq_len//16 (128)
column4: positive — reducing sliding window from seq_len//8 (256) to seq_len//16 (128) improved val_bpb from 0.993565 to 0.991270 (δ=-0.002295). this is the single largest improvement from any experiment in the project — larger than the 1024→512 step (-0.000990). simple 1-line change, no complexity added. vram increased slightly to 50272 mb (within budget). committed and pushed to autoresearch/run1.

column2: Further reduce sliding window size from seq_len//16 (128) to seq_len//32 (64)
column4: discard — reducing sliding window from seq_len//16 (128) to seq_len//32 (64) worsened val_bpb from 0.991270 to 0.992390 (δ=+0.001120). window size 64 is too narrow — the model can no longer capture sufficient local context patterns with only 64 tokens of sliding window. the accelerating improvement trend from window reduction has reversed. 128 (seq_len//16) appears to be near-optimal. reverted train.py, no push.

column2: Try intermediate sliding window size seq_len//24 (~85 tokens) between the optimal 128 and failed 64
column4: discard — worse than baseline, confirms 128 is optimal window size

column2: Increase batch size (try 2x current value) to test gradient quality vs step count tradeoff
Halve batch size from 2**19 (524K) to 2**18 (262K) to maximize training steps
column4: positive — halving batch size from 2**19 (524k) to 2**18 (262k) improved val_bpb from 0.991270 to 0.982098 (delta -0.009172). this confirms step-maximization as a primary optimization axis: ~1780 steps at ~170ms/step vs ~900 steps at ~330ms/step. vram decreased slightly to 50.1gb. committed and pushed to autoresearch/run1.

column2: Further halve batch size from 2**18 (262K) to 2**17 (131K) to double training steps
Re-tune learning rates for halved batch size: scale all LRs by 0.707x (sqrt scaling rule)
column4: positive — val_bpb improved from 0.982098 to 0.981876 (δ=-0.000222). pure hyperparameter change: scaled all lrs by 0.707x (sqrt scaling rule) for the halved batch size. zero complexity added, zero vram change (vram 25325→50100 mb due to batch size regime, not this change). committed to autoresearch/run1.

column2: Reduce MLP expansion ratio to speed up per-step compute and increase total training steps
Reduce RoPE theta to improve positional resolution for 128-token sliding window
Eliminate warmup entirely (WARMUP_RATIO 0.05 → 0.0) to maximize steps at peak LR
Increase cooldown ratio from 0.5 to 0.7 (exploit the strong cooldown-sensitivity signal)
column4: positive — increasing warmdown_ratio from 0.5 to 0.7 improved val_bpb from 0.981876 to 0.981747 (δ=-0.000129). the longer cooldown phase (70% of training steps decaying lr vs 50%) provides a smoother descent that improves final convergence. simple one-line constant change, zero complexity added. vram unchanged at 50100 mb. committed and pushed to autoresearch/run1.

column2: Increase cooldown ratio from 0.7 to 0.8 (continue cooldown optimization trend)
Double attention heads (4→8) by halving HEAD_DIM from 128 to 64
column4: negative. head_dim=64 (8 heads) regressed val_bpb from 0.981747 to 0.985453 (+0.003706). doubling attention heads from 4→8 while halving head dimension from 128→64 hurt performance at this model scale. the 4-head×128-dim configuration is better suited for n_embd=512. reverted train.py, no code pushed.

column2: Change MLP activation function (try squared ReLU or alternative to current activation)
Re-test batch size 2^17 (131K) with properly sqrt-scaled learning rates (0.707x current)
column4: positive — val_bpb improved from 0.981747 to 0.981081 (δ=-0.000666). batch size halved to 2^17 (131k) with sqrt-scaled lrs (0.707x) successfully addresses the lr/batch mismatch that caused the original 2^17 test to fail. the 2x more training steps (3418 vs ~1780) at ~88ms/step with properly dampened lrs yielded a clear improvement. vram dropped to 25.3gb (from ~48gb with 2^18). committed and pushed to autoresearch/run1.

column2: Further halve batch size to 2^16 (65K) with sqrt-scaled LRs (0.707x current)
Re-tune weight decay from 0.1 to 0.07 for current reduced-LR / reduced-batch regime
column4: keep — val_bpb improved from 0.981081 to 0.981052 (δ=-0.000029). reduced weight_decay from 0.1 to 0.07. zero complexity cost (one constant change). the reduced regularization benefits the current low-lr, small-batch regime as hypothesized.

column2: Change warmdown LR schedule shape from linear to cosine decay
Explore SSSL layer parameters (state dimension or expansion factor)
Add minimum LR floor (10% of peak) to prevent final warmdown steps from decaying to zero
Increase RoPE theta from 10000 to 50000 (test higher positional base frequency)
column4: positive — rope theta 10000→50000 improved val_bpb from 0.981052 to 0.980603 (δ=-0.000449). single constant change, zero complexity, zero vram impact. kept and pushed.

column2: Increase RoPE theta from 50000 to 100000 (continue higher positional frequency trend)
column4: positive: increasing rope theta from 50000 to 100000 improved val_bpb from 0.980603 to 0.980499 (δ=-0.000104). zero-complexity constant change — the higher theta trend continues to yield improvement, though with diminishing returns compared to the 10000→50000 jump (δ=-0.000449). the optimal rope theta may be even higher, or we may be approaching the sweet spot.

column2: Increase RoPE theta from 100000 to 200000 (continue diminishing-returns exploration)
column4: negative. rope theta 200000 produced val_bpb=0.980833, which is worse than the current best of 0.980499 (δ=+0.000334). the diminishing-returns curve has reversed — increasing theta beyond 100000 hurts performance. optimal rope theta is bracketed at 100000. reverted train.py, no push.

column2: Increase full-attention layers by changing WINDOW_PATTERN from "SSSL" to "SSL" (3 L-layers vs 2)
column4: positive — val_bpb improved from 0.980499 to 0.978752 (−0.001747). changing window_pattern from "sssl" to "ssl" increases full-attention layers from ~2 to 3 per 9 layers. despite slightly fewer total steps (3417 vs ~3400 baseline), the richer attention capacity more than compensated. step time stayed at ~88ms (no significant overhead). vram 25.3gb, well within budget.

column2: Change WINDOW_PATTERN from "SSL" to "SL" (4 full-attention layers per 9 instead of 3)
Tie input and output embedding weights to reduce parameters and increase throughput
Re-tune learning rates: increase all peak LRs by 1.3x to test if sqrt batch-scaling was overcorrective
column4: positive — val_bpb improved from 0.978752 to 0.978107 (δ=-0.000645). all peak lrs increased by 1.3x, confirming that sqrt batch-scaling was overcorrective for the current architecture. zero-complexity constant change, compounding benefit for all future experiments.

column2: Push peak learning rates to 1.5x of current values (continue LR re-tuning after successful 1.3x)
column4: discard - val_bpb increased (worse), confirms 1.5x overshoots optimal lr

column2: Binary search LR multiplier: try 1.4x (midpoint between 1.3x keep and 1.5x discard)
column4: keep. 1.4x lr multiplier improved val_bpb from 0.978107 to 0.977651 (δ=-0.000456). zero complexity change — pure constant updates. optimal lr range narrows to [1.4x, 1.5x).

column2: Reduce gradient clipping max_norm from 1.0 to 0.5 (tighter clipping for noisy small-batch regime)
Binary search LR multiplier: try 1.45x (midpoint between 1.4x keep and 1.5x discard)
Add EMA (Exponential Moving Average) of model weights for evaluation (decay=0.999)
Reorder WINDOW_PATTERN from "SSL" to "LSS" (full-attention at bottom of stack instead of top)
Tune RMSNorm epsilon (try increasing from default 1e-8 to 1e-5)
Halve Muon momentum warmup from 300 steps to 150 steps (maximize time at peak momentum 0.95)
Increase Muon momentum warmup from 300 to 500 steps (extend gradual momentum ramp)
Increase Muon initial momentum from 0.85 to 0.90 (narrower warmup range, higher initial efficiency)
Re-test depth 10 (with adjusted ASPECT_RATIO to keep n_embd=512) at the current small-batch regime (2^17)
Increase MLP expansion ratio from 4x to 5x (test whether additional MLP capacity improves val_bpb)
column4: positive — increasing mlp expansion ratio from 4x to 5x improved val_bpb from 0.977651 to 0.975873 (δ = -0.001778). the additional mlp capacity outweighs the ~7.5% step-count reduction (3145 vs ~3400 steps). vram increased modestly from ~25.3gb to 27.7gb, well within the 70gb soft cap.

column2: Increase MLP expansion ratio from 5x to 6x (continue capacity scaling after strong 5x result)
column4: discard — val_bpb increased, confirming 5x is the optimal mlp expansion ratio

column2: Re-tune learning rates for 5x MLP: increase all peak LRs by 1.15x
column4: positive — increasing all 4 peak lrs by 1.15x improved val_bpb from 0.975873 to 0.975663 (δ=-0.000210). the modest lr boost compensates for the 5x mlp's reduced step count and different gradient distributions. zero complexity cost — pure constant changes. vram unchanged at 27.7gb. committed and pushed to autoresearch/run1.

column2: Re-tune weight decay from 0.07 to 0.05 for the 5x MLP regime
column4: positive: reducing weight_decay from 0.07 to 0.05 improved val_bpb from 0.975663 to 0.975180 (δ=-0.000483). the 5x mlp regime benefits from less regularization, as hypothesized.

column2: Further reduce weight decay from 0.05 to 0.03 (continue less-regularization trend for 5x MLP)
column4: discard — reducing weight_decay from 0.05 to 0.03 worsened val_bpb from 0.975180 to 0.976281 (δ=+0.001101). despite the hypothesis that less regularization would help the 5x mlp regime, 0.03 is too low — the model begins to overfit without sufficient weight decay. the previous reduction from 0.07→0.05 (exp 60, δ=-0.000483) was beneficial, but 0.05→0.03 reverses the trend. this confirms 0.05 as the optimal weight decay for the current 5x mlp regime. reverted train.py, no code pushed.

column2: Differential LR: increase only MATRIX_LR by 1.2x (test independent tuning of main weight LR)
column4: negative. increasing matrix_lr by 1.2x (0.0483 → 0.05796) while keeping other lrs constant resulted in val_bpb=0.976361, which is +0.001181 worse than the baseline of 0.975180. the differential lr hypothesis for matrix parameters did not help — the higher muon lr slightly destabilized training. reverted train.py, no code pushed.

column2: Add QK-Norm (Query-Key Normalization) to stabilize attention with high learning rates
column3: N/A — no run needed, change already in baseline
column4: no-op: qk-norm is already present in the baseline. the current train.py already applies rmsnorm to both q and k vectors after rotary embeddings (line: `q, k = norm(q), norm(k)` in causalselfattention.forward). the proposed change duplicates existing functionality — no code modification or training run was needed.

column2: Try Grouped Query Attention (GQA) with 2 KV heads (n_kv_heads=2, sharing across 4 Q heads)
Add Z-loss regularization to stabilize logit magnitudes (alpha=1e-4)
Increase logit softcap from 15 to 30 (double output dynamic range for 50K vocabulary)
Reduce logit softcap from 15 to 10 (tighter output regularization for 5x MLP)
column4: negative — reducing logit softcap from 15 to 10 slightly worsened val_bpb (0.975309 vs 0.975180 baseline, +0.000129). the tighter constraint hurt rather than helped. combined with experiment 67's result (softcap 30 → +0.004235), this confirms softcap=15 is near-optimal. softcap direction is now exhausted in both directions. reverted train.py, no push.

column2: Reduce AdamW beta2 from 0.999 to 0.95 for embedding/unembedding optimizer (faster variance adaptation)
column4: no-op: adam_betas is already (0.8, 0.95) at line 444 of train.py. the beta2 for embedding/unembedding adamw optimizer is already 0.95, not the assumed default 0.999. no code change was made.

column2: Optimize torch.compile settings (try mode="max-autotune" for faster per-step throughput)
column4: positive: val_bpb improved from 0.975180 to 0.975012 (delta: -0.000168). changed torch.compile to mode="max-autotune-no-cudagraphs" which enabled autotuned cuda kernel selection, yielding ~80 additional training steps (3225 vs ~3145 baseline). note: mode="max-autotune" (with cuda graphs) caused a segfault during autotuning, so the no-cudagraphs variant was used instead.

column2: Binary search MLP expansion ratio: try 5.5x (midpoint between 5x keep and 6x discard)
Binary search MLP expansion: try 5.75x (midpoint between 5.5x keep and 6x discard)
column4: discard — mlp expansion 5.75x yields val_bpb=0.974408, worse than baseline 0.974124 (δ=+0.000284). the extra capacity at 5.75x does not outweigh the ~2% step-count loss vs 5.5x. optimal mlp expansion lies in [5.5, 5.75). reverted train.py, no push.

column2: Add stochastic depth (layer dropout) with survival probability 0.9 during training
Change AdamW beta1 from 0.8 to 0.9 for embedding/unembedding optimizer (more gradient smoothing)
Add label smoothing (coefficient 0.1) to cross-entropy loss for improved generalization
column4: negative — label smoothing hurts val_bpb in this 5-minute training regime. tried α=0.1 (val_bpb 1.0118, +3.9%) and α=0.05 (val_bpb 0.9919, +1.8%), both significantly worse than baseline 0.974124. the softcap=15 already provides logit regularization; label smoothing's target-side smoothing appears counterproductive here, possibly because it forces the model to allocate probability mass to incorrect tokens during the limited ~400m token training budget, reducing effective learning signal.

column2: Test WINDOW_PATTERN "SLS" (evenly-distributed full-attention at positions 2,5,8 vs current "SSL" at 3,6,9)
column4: negative — window_pattern "sls" (val_bpb 0.975868) is worse than current best "ssl" (val_bpb 0.974124). δ=+0.001744. reverted, not pushed.

column2: Add Stochastic Weight Averaging (SWA) over the last 20% of training steps
column4: negative. swa (tail averaging last 20% of steps) produced val_bpb=0.975675 vs baseline 0.974124 — worse by +0.001551. the averaged weights degraded performance, suggesting the final checkpoint is already better than any uniform average over the last 20% of training. the model's warmdown schedule (cosine decay of lr over last 70%) likely already drives convergence to a good minimum, making swa redundant or harmful. reverted train.py, no code pushed.

column2: Re-tune peak learning rates by 1.1x for the 5.5x MLP regime
column4: positive — val_bpb improved from 0.974124 to 0.973849 (δ=-0.000275). all four peak learning rates increased by 1.1x for the 5.5x mlp regime. zero complexity cost (4 constant changes only). vram unchanged at ~28.9 gb.

column2: Parallel attention + MLP blocks (PaLM-style: x + attn(norm(x)) + mlp(norm(x)))
Re-tune warmdown ratio from 0.7 to 0.75 for 5.5x MLP + higher LR regime
Tune AdamW epsilon from 1e-8 to 1e-6 for embedding/unembedding optimizer
Reduce EMBEDDING_LR by 0.85x independently (test differential embedding learning rate)
Reduce UNEMBEDDING_LR by 0.85x independently (parallel to successful EMBEDDING_LR reduction)
Reduce SCALAR_LR by 0.85x independently (differential scalar parameter LR tuning)
column4: positive — reducing scalar_lr by 0.85x (0.664125 → 0.56450625) improved val_bpb from 0.973612 to 0.972794 (δ=-0.000818). this confirms scalar parameters (biases, normalization scale factors) were also over-scaled by the compound lr adjustments, similar to the embedding lr finding. zero complexity cost — single constant change.

column2: Reduce MATRIX_LR by 0.85x independently (complete differential LR tuning for Muon-optimized weights)
Further reduce SCALAR_LR by 0.85x (compound to ~0.72x of pre-differential-tuning value)
Apply depth-scaled initialization for residual-path output weights (1/sqrt(2*n_layers) scaling)
column4: negative — depth-scaled init for residual-path output weights (1/sqrt(2*n_layers) scaling) degraded val_bpb from 0.972794 to 0.977241. the existing zero-init for attn.c_proj and mlp.c_proj is a stronger stabilization approach for this architecture, as blocks start as identity mappings and gradually learn non-trivial transformations. reverted train.py, no code pushed.

column2: Replace squared ReLU MLP with SwiGLU (gated activation) at compute-matched hidden dimension
column4: negative — swiglu mlp (hidden dim 1920, 3 weight matrices) performed worse than squared relu baseline. val_bpb: 0.980074 vs baseline 0.972794 (+0.007 worse). the 3-matrix swiglu structure increased step time from ~95ms to ~103ms, reducing total steps from ~3200 to ~2950 in the 5-minute budget. the per-step quality gain from swiglu was insufficient to overcome the ~8% step count reduction. reverted train.py, no push.

column2: Add weight decay warmup schedule (ramp WD from 0→0.05 over first 10% of training steps)
Use constant weight decay (0.05) instead of linearly decaying schedule (WD*(1-progress))
Reduce token embedding initialization std from 1.0 to 0.1 (test initialization scale impact)
Cyclical LR with 2 warm restart cycles (SGDR) to escape local minimum
column4: negative — sgdr (2 warm restart cycles) degraded val_bpb from 0.972794 to 0.984487. the lr restart at 50% training caused a massive loss spike (~2.84 → ~3.00) that the model couldn't recover from in the remaining half of training. the second cycle only reached loss levels comparable to where the first cycle was at ~60% through, wasting ~120s of training time on re-learning. in this short 5-minute training regime, the cost of restarting from peak lr far outweighs any potential benefit from escaping local minima. reverted train.py, no code pushed.

column2: Reduce Muon optimizer Newton-Schulz iterations from 5 to 3 (trade orthogonalization precision for throughput)
Increase Muon Newton-Schulz iterations from 5 to 7 (test higher orthogonalization precision)
Apply depth-wise MATRIX_LR scaling: earlier layers 0.85x, later layers 1.15x (linear interpolation)
Add Value normalization (V-norm) alongside existing QK-norm in attention
Set weight decay to 0 for embedding and unembedding parameter groups (exempt lookup tables from L2 regularization)
column4: no-op: embedding (wte) and unembedding (lm_head) parameter groups already have weight_decay=0.0 in setup_optimizer(). only muon groups (matrix params from transformer.h) receive the non-zero weight_decay=0.05 with decaying schedule. the training loop also only updates weight_decay for kind='muon' groups. the proposed change is already the current state of the code — no modification was needed or made.

column2: Add multi-token prediction: auxiliary head predicts token t+2 from position t (0.3× loss weight)
column4: negative: val_bpb=2.036507, massively worse than baseline 0.972794. multi-token prediction (auxiliary head predicting t+2 with 0.3× loss weight) catastrophically degraded training. the 0.3× auxiliary loss overwhelmed the primary training signal — combined loss stayed in the 5.7-6.0 range throughout training instead of converging to the expected ~3.5-4.0 range. additionally, throughput dropped from ~3200 to 2772 steps due to extra linear layer + matmul. first attempt crashed (torch.compile dynamic=false shape mismatch from slicing); fixed by using same-shape tensors with ignore_index masking. even with the fix, the fundamental approach is harmful at this model scale and training budget. the auxiliary loss weight of 0.3 is far too high — it essentially turns the model into a multi-task learner where the secondary task dominates gradients. discarded — reverted train.py.

column2: Concentrate all full-attention at final 3 layers: WINDOW_PATTERN "SSSSSSLLL" (layers 6-8 full, layers 0-5 sliding window)
Reduce Muon initial momentum from 0.85 to 0.80 (wider momentum warmup range for early exploration)
Reduce UNEMBEDDING_LR by 0.95x (gentle 5% reduction, following near-miss Exp 84)
